creator: cvs-src@yandex.ru
text:
03.01.2011 13:38, Thomas Sandford Ð¿Ð¸ÑÐµÑ:
>>
>
> But will the 6.x Drush version also work with drupal 5.X (for which
> there is also a FreeBSD port)? I don't want to break the drush port for
> existing users of either drupal port.
>
> Could you point me at the onsite (drush) documentation where the
> cross-compatibility between drupal versions is documented so I can
> increase my understanding of this issue. I did try to find this when the
> bug was first opened but couldn't find anything relevant.
>
> --

http://drupal.org/project/drush
"""
It is valid to use the latest 'All Versions' release (or HEAD) no matter 
what your version of Drupal is. Drush is independent of Drupal version :)
"""

drush-6.x-3.3.tar.gz is actually All-versions-3.3, they just change 
tarball name.

 From README.txt:
http://drupalcode.org/viewvc/drupal/contributions/modules/drush/README.txt?view=markup
"""
Drush works with Drupal 5, Drupal 6 and Drupal 7.
"""

So it's safe to update to 3.3 and (when stable version will be released) 
to 4.0.

-- 
Regards,
Ruslan
